title: Multi-Instance Salesforce Object and Metadata Fetching
story: As a Salesforce administrator, I want to authenticate and connect additional Salesforce instances so that I can browse their objects and metadata and map them directly to Salesforce objects instead of creating custom source objects.
description:
## Context

Organizations using multiple Salesforce instances need a way to connect each instance to DataTools Pro, browse its objects, and inspect metadata — all without manually creating custom source objects.

## Behavior

### Authentication
- Users initiate an OAuth flow to authenticate a second (or additional) Salesforce instance.
- Each instance is uniquely identified by its `instance_url`. Attempting to add a duplicate instance returns an error prompting the user to connect a different one.
- On successful OAuth callback, access tokens are saved and associated with the current team.

### Object and Metadata Fetching
- When a new instance is first connected, background jobs fetch all Salesforce objects for that instance.
- Metadata for each object is also fetched via batched background jobs using Laravel queues. Objects are chunked to avoid API overload.
- This bulk fetch runs only once — on initial instance connection.
- Users can later view object lists and drill into individual object metadata.

### Data Model
- **SalesforceSecondInstanceUserInformation** — represents a connected Salesforce instance, scoped to a team. Stores OAuth credentials and instance URL.
- **SalesforceInstanceObject** — individual Salesforce objects belonging to a connected instance.
- **SalesforceInstanceObjectMetaData** — metadata fields for each object, fetched from the Salesforce API.

### Controller Endpoints
- `redirectToSalesforce()` / `callback()` — OAuth initiation and callback handling.
- `listInstance()` / `tableListInstance()` — list all connected instances for the current team.
- `instnaceObjects($instance_id)` / `instnaceObjectsTable($instance_id)` — view objects for a specific instance.
- `instnaceObjectsMetaData($instance_id, $object_id)` / `listtableMetaData($instance_id, $object_id)` — view metadata for a specific object.
- `fetchObjectsFromSalesforceInBackGround()` — dispatches background jobs to fetch objects.
- `fetchObjectsMetaDataFromSalesforceInBackGround()` — dispatches background jobs to fetch object metadata.

### Queue Configuration
Requires `QUEUE_CONNECTION=database` in `.env`, the `queue:table` migration, and a running queue worker (`php artisan queue:listen`).
acceptance_criteria: User can initiate OAuth to connect a new Salesforce instance
System prevents adding a duplicate instance (same instance_url) and shows an error message
On first connection, all objects for the new instance are fetched via background jobs
Metadata for each object is fetched in batches via queued jobs to avoid API overload
User can view a list of all connected Salesforce instances for their team
User can browse objects belonging to a selected instance
User can drill into an object to view its metadata fields
Background fetch of objects and metadata runs only on initial instance connection
Access tokens from OAuth callback are persisted and scoped to the team
ux_changes: New screens for adding a Salesforce instance via OAuth, listing connected instances, browsing objects per instance, and viewing object metadata in table format.
middleware_changes: SalesforceSecondInstanceController handles OAuth flow, instance listing, object/metadata browsing, and dispatching Laravel queue jobs for background fetching. Batch processing chunks objects to prevent API overload.
data_model_changes: Three new Eloquent models: SalesforceSecondInstanceUserInformation (instance credentials scoped to team), SalesforceInstanceObject (objects per instance), and SalesforceInstanceObjectMetaData (metadata per object). One-to-many relationships chain instance → objects → metadata.
api_changes: New controller routes: OAuth redirect/callback, instance listing (JSON and table), object listing per instance, metadata listing per object, and background fetch triggers for objects and metadata.
implementation_notes: Uses Laravel Jobs and Queues for background processing. Requires QUEUE_CONNECTION=database and a running queue worker. Objects are chunked into batches before dispatching jobs to avoid Salesforce API rate limits. The bulk metadata fetch runs only once on initial instance connection.
comment_summary: Developer provided a detailed technical walkthrough of the implementation including database models, controller methods, batch processing strategy, and queue configuration. Feature was developed on a local branch and not merged to development at the time of comment to avoid conflicts.
